findings.description: fetch_events docstring (line 32) promises a closed interval '[start, end] — both boundary days included', but the SQL uses 'created_at < end.isoformat()' (midnight of end day), silently excluding every event on the end day — an off-by-one-day under-report contradicting the stated contract.
top_users replicates the same half-open 'created_at < end' boundary while sharing the codebase's documented 'inclusive end day' intent, so the leaderboard omits the final day's activity.
event_count uses 'created_at < end' (both action and no-action branches, lines 82 and 88), excluding the end day and producing counts inconsistent with the inclusive-boundary contract.

render_activity_report's 'Total events' (len(events) from an INNER-JOIN query) and event_count() (queries events table with NO join) count different populations: orphaned events with no matching users row are dropped by fetch_events/top_users but counted by event_count, so two 'event count' APIs for the same window can disagree.

display_name and action are interpolated into HTML via f-strings with no escaping, so even benign data containing '<', '>' or '&' (e.g. 'A&B Corp') corrupts the table markup and yields malformed HTML — a correctness defect independent of the XSS/security angle (defer exploit detail to critic-security).

top_users does not validate 'limit'; passing limit=-1 makes SQLite treat 'LIMIT -1' as unlimited (returns the entire grouped set), silently violating the 'top N' contract, and limit=0 returns nothing.

_connect uses sqlite3.connect which creates an empty DB file for a missing path, so every public method then raises a raw 'no such table: events' OperationalError with no graceful handling or clear error — neither validation nor degradation.

render_activity_report has no LIMIT (unlike top_users): fetch_events .fetchall() loads all rows into memory and the report is built by repeated 'rows +=' string concatenation, scaling unboundedly with the date range (defer detail to critic-performance).

save_report opens the file with mode 'w' and no error handling; an invalid out_path, permission error, or full disk propagates a raw OSError and may leave a truncated/empty file, with no atomic write or cleanup.

PAGE_TEMPLATE.format(...) parses only the literal template, so brace characters in title/rows argument values are not re-scanned — low residual risk, noted for completeness; a templating engine with autoescaping would be safer than str.format over an HTML string.
